text: Arjun — the transition into the second figure of the Ballyvourney reel, I want projections to drop to near-dark there. Just the floor map, nothing on the back flat. Can you pull up what we had from March?
On it. The March file had two versions — amber wash bleeding in at bar 8, or clean. Which were you thinking?
Clean. Siobhán's footwork gets buried if there's warm light competing. She's leading that section now so it needs to read.
Got it. I'll rework the timing cue and send a test render tonight — should land before your lunch tomorrow.
Grand. Brendan's collecting Declan after school so I'll actually have time to sit with it properly. Send whenever.
Venkatesan — stage is 20 ft wide not 22. Treble Reel will pull two from the back line. Lighting plot needs updating before Thursday.
Confirmed. I will push the revised footprint into the rigging schedule today. Pushti, please regenerate the cue list.
On it tonight. Will share revised cue list by 9 PM IST.
New Earth-imagery references from NASA dropped into Box. Look at the Kerala cloudbands shot — feels like Sliabh Luachra opening.
Saw them. Pulling the cloudband into the moodboard now. Want to keep the warmth or grade cooler?
Logan — Riverside floor map for the solo recital. Same configuration as last December, no risers.
Noted. Dr. Richardson has signed off on the technical sheet. Stage door open from 4 PM IST on Nov 22.
Kalaivanar mini hall confirmed for Oct 24. Eight on stage opening céilí set, twelve for ensemble close.
Cues received. Will run focus check Thursday morning.
Emma's solo programme order is locked. Five pieces, twenty minutes, no interval.
Kathleen is happy. Ticket allocation goes live Monday.
Patrick's bodhrán take is locked at 114 BPM for the second reel. Aoife's vocal lift sits on top cleanly.
Good. Send the bounce to Margaret in Cork for Thursday's review.
Dec 15 lecture-demonstration — academic abstract draft attached. Margaret will review.
Weekly sync moved to 4 PM IST Friday. Calendar invites going out.
Arjun, one more on the Ballyvourney reel — the near-dark hold shouldn't run past bar 12. Siobhán steps into the diagonal there and I want the floor map lifting back up as she crosses centre.
Watched the test render twice this morning. The clean version is exactly right, but the fade back in feels a half-beat late against Patrick's bodhrán. Can we pull it forward slightly?
Grand work on the timing. That's the first time the footwork has actually read from the back rows in that section.
Small note for the review — when we drop to floor map only, keep the intensity around forty percent. Full black swallows the dancers' feet and the whole point is that we see the batter clearly.
The back flat staying bare is the right call. Anything projected there and the eye leaves the dancers, which is the opposite of what this reel needs.
Venkatesan, confirming the twenty-foot width has gone into every plot now? I don't want the wing masking cut for the old twenty-two.
Pushti, the revised cue list looks solid. One correction — cue 14 should fire on the second bar of the treble reel, not the first, or the dancers arrive before the light does.
The two we're pulling from the back line will enter from stage right instead. Please update the traffic plan so we don't have a collision in the wing.
For the Water element I keep coming back to that cloudband over the coast — it has the same slow pull as a Sliabh Luachra slow air. Let's build the whole section's tempo off that image.
Grade it cooler for Water, but hold the warmth for the Earth section. The two elements should feel like different times of day, not just different colours.
Logan, same floor as last December is grand, but nudge the entrance mark a foot upstage. The soloist was clipping the front edge on her opening turn last time.
No risers is right for a solo recital — keeps everything intimate and the footwork close to the audience. Let's hold that.
Kalaivanar's mini hall is shallow, so the eight in the opening céilí set need tighter spacing than we rehearsed. Let's take a foot out of every gap.
For the ensemble close of twelve, we'll stagger entrances from both wings or they'll never all fit on at once in that space.
Emma's five pieces are well ordered — the slow air sitting third gives her a breath before the two fast reels close it out. Don't move it.
For her debut I want the lighting simple and warm. This is her first solo programme and the audience should be watching her, not a light show.
The bodhrán at 114 for the second reel is spot on, but check the tempo doesn't drift up in the last eight bars — Patrick tends to lean forward with the excitement.
Aoife's vocal lift is beautiful, though it's sitting a touch loud over the fiddle in the bridge. Pull her back two decibels and let the strings carry.
For the lecture-demonstration I'd like two dancers on hand to illustrate the difference between sean-nós and the modern competitive step. Nothing shows it like seeing them side by side.
Reminder the studio's closed the last week of December for the holidays — plan any renders or bounces you need before then, as I'll be offline with the family.
Can you flag which reels still have the amber wash baked in from the old file? I want a clean sweep before we lock anything for the full company run.
Declan's home with a cold so I'm working from the kitchen table today, but I've got eyes on Slack. Send the revised cue sheet whenever it's ready.
The transition out of the second figure looks tidy now. My only worry is the corner dancers — from house left they'll be right at the edge of the light pool. Widen it a touch?
Let's not overthink the opening. Simple floor map, dancers enter in near-dark, light builds with the tune. That restraint is what will make the finale land.
I sat with the full sequence last night once the kids were down. It flows. The only rough patch is the handover between the reel and the slow air — projections change too abruptly there.
For a civic inauguration the sightlines matter more than usual — dignitaries sit dead centre. Keep the strongest formation moments squared to that seat.
On it. I'll walk the revised footprint with the dancers first thing so nobody's guessing at spacing on the day.
Venkatesan, is the rigging schedule allowing time for a full focus check, or are we lighting blind at the tech? I'd rather lose an hour now than scramble.
Fire is the one I'm least sure of. The NASA volcanic imagery is stunning but it risks overpowering the dancers. Maybe we imply fire through light rather than projection?
Air should almost be an absence — pale sky, high cloud, plenty of negative space so the leaps read against emptiness. Restraint again.
Please confirm with Dr. Richardson that the stage-door open time works for the accompanist too. The fiddler likes a full hour to settle and tune.
Logan, is the Riverside floor sprung or flat? If it's flat we'll want the marley down early so her feet aren't taking a beating through rehearsal.
The focus check on Thursday — please include the downstage edge specially. That hall's front lights spill into the first row of seats if we're not careful.
The hall's ceiling is low, so keep the top light shallow. Steep angles there just carve shadows under the dancers' eyes.
Kathleen, once ticket allocation is live, hold six in the second row for Emma's family. They've travelled from Cork and I want them close.
Emma, your opening hornpipe — take the first eight bars slower than you think you need. Let the room settle on you before you lift the tempo.
For the opening slow air, can we get the fiddle a hair drier? The current reverb tail smears the footwork cue and the dancers can't find the downbeat.
Margaret in Cork will want the stems as well as the bounce so she can check the balance on her own monitors. Send both when you post Thursday's review.
The academic abstract reads well, but let's soften the second paragraph — it's a general audience, not a conference, so ease off the theory and lean into the storytelling.
Could everyone add their availability for the January company block to the shared sheet by Friday? I'd like to lock the rehearsal calendar before term starts.
For the slow air, could we try a very slow cross-fade instead of a hard cut? The music breathes into it, and the visuals should do the same.
Arjun, remind me — is the back-line entrance cue tied to the music timecode or are we still calling it manually? I'd rather it be locked to the track for the review.
The Kerala cloudband reference you pulled works beautifully behind the slow section. Warm enough to feel like home, not so warm it fights the footwork lighting.
Venkatesan asked whether we can share the near-dark timing with the Metro team since they liked the effect. I'm happy to, once we've signed off here first.
Honestly the biggest improvement this round is the restraint. We stopped trying to fill every surface and the dancing has room to speak now.
The opening céilí set needs to read as celebratory from the very first beat — this is a public occasion, not a recital. Bright, open, generous formations.
Pushti, please add a hold at the end of the treble reel for applause. Civic crowds clap long and we don't want the next number stepping on it.
Confirmed the stage is raised eighteen inches at the front lip. Warn the dancers — the treble reel batter near the edge needs care or someone turns an ankle.
Earth wants the deep greens of the delta shots. That's where Siobhán's grounded footwork lives, so the visuals should feel rooted, not floating.
Let's order the five as Earth, Water, Air, Fire, and then a spirit coda that pulls a fragment from each. Gives the whole piece an arc rather than five separate ideas.
One follow spot only for this recital. It's a solo — anything more and we're dressing up something that should feel bare and honest.
The technical sheet looks complete. My only addition is a chair and water set discreetly stage left for the interval breath between the third and fourth pieces.
Confirm the AV feed for the projections runs off the house desk and not our laptop. Last time at a civic hall the switchover cost us ten minutes we didn't have.
Eight on for the opening, twelve for the close, and a clean blackout between to reset formation. Keep that blackout under four seconds or the energy drops.
Twenty minutes with no interval is the right length for a debut. Long enough to show range, short enough that she finishes wanting more, not exhausted.
We'll do one full dress run the evening before, then leave her be. Over-rehearsing a debut just feeds the nerves.
The click track for rehearsal only, please — never in the theatre. These dancers phrase to the living breath of the tune, not a metronome.
There's a click of a chair or something at the top of Aoife's take around bar three. Small thing, but on a quiet opening it'll carry. Can we clean it?
Margaret's happy for us to use the Cork field recordings in the demo as long as we credit the singers by name on the slide. Please make sure that's in.
Brendan's set up a shared drive for all the show files so we stop emailing versions back and forth. Link's pinned to this channel — please move your working files over.
Can we get one render at performance brightness rather than screen brightness? The hall projector is dimmer than my monitor and I keep misjudging the floor map.
I've marked up the storyboard where the formation splits into two lines. The projection seam needs to sit dead centre or the symmetry breaks.
Brendan reminded me the review room only has the short-throw projector, so keep the floor map within a four-metre square or it'll clip at the edges.
Let's keep tomorrow's review to the reel and the slow air only. If we try to cover the whole show we'll rush both and decide nothing properly.
Emma's coming in to watch the review — she's curious how the near-dark hold works before her own solo lighting. Grand if she sits in?
I've capped the ensemble at fourteen for the finale. Any more and the twenty-foot width turns the whole thing into a scramble.
Venkatesan, the sound team wants a monitor upstage for the fiddle. Fine by me as long as it doesn't eat into the back line's running space.
Grand, the revised footprint works. I walked it this morning and the two-from-the-back adjustment actually tightens the picture rather than thinning it.
The transition between elements is where this lives or dies. I'd like each one to dissolve into the next like weather changing, no hard scene breaks.
Arjun, can you pull the highest-resolution version of the delta shot? At show scale the current one softens too much and loses the texture I want behind Earth.
Brendan's driving me over so I'll be there for the four o'clock door. Give me twenty minutes to walk the floor before the soloist arrives.
Grand, that's Riverside sorted. Same configuration, one spot, marley down early, and the door at four. Thanks Logan, tidy as ever.
Please check the wing space either side — if it's as narrow as I remember, the twelve for the close will be queuing in the corridor. Plan the hold accordingly.
Grand, that's the Kalaivanar tech plan set. Focus check Thursday morning, house desk drives the AV, and tighter spacing throughout. On we go.
Kathleen, please make sure the programme spells her name properly and lists the tune names in Irish with the English underneath. It matters to her family.
That's Emma's debut locked — five pieces, twenty minutes, warm light, family in row two. She's ready. This is going to be a lovely night for her.
Let's lock the running order of the audio so it matches the show — slow air, first reel, bridge, second reel at 114. That way the bounce plays straight through at the tech.
Grand, that's the audio in good shape. Bodhrán locked, Aoife balanced, fiddle drier on the air, stems off to Margaret. Lovely work, Patrick.
Keep the whole demonstration to forty minutes with ten for questions. Any longer and even a keen audience starts to drift. Tight and warm is the aim.
Lovely run this evening, everyone. Go home, rest your feet, and we'll pick it up fresh at Friday's four o'clock sync.
The floor map's colour temperature drifted cooler between versions two and three. Not a complaint, just check it's intentional before we call it final.
One thing I love in the latest cut — you left a breath of silence and stillness before the first projection appears. Don't lose that, it sets the whole tone.
If we're rendering overnight, prioritise the reel transition. That's the section the company sees first at Thursday's run and I want it right.
Arjun, I've moved my notes into the shared doc so we're not scrolling back through the thread. Everything from this pass is on the second tab.
That's us then — reel and slow air locked pending the performance-brightness render. Lovely work all round, genuinely.
For the inauguration we'll need a clean cut-off at the run time — the officials have a tight schedule after. Let's rehearse to the clock, not just the music.
Pushti, send me the final cue list as a PDF once cue 14 is fixed. I'll print it and mark my own calls for the desk.
That's the plot, the footprint, and the cue list all aligned now. Well done everyone — this one came together faster than I feared.
I want the audience to feel the elements before they name them. If someone in the front row says that felt like rain, we've done our job.
Let's keep a human scale throughout — these are cosmic images but it's a dance about people. The projections serve the bodies, never the other way round.
